# Macrotide

> An open-source AI investment companion for Thai index investors. A single
> Next.js process (App Router, React 19, TypeScript) over a local SQLite file
> (Drizzle ORM), with passkey auth (better-auth), an "Advisor" chat built on the
> Vercel AI SDK via OpenRouter, and live market data. Personal-use experiment;
> not investment advice.

This file is a curated map for LLMs and coding agents. Read the linked docs
on demand rather than crawling the whole repo. Paths are relative to the repo
root so they resolve when browsing the source.

## Start here

- [Root README](./README.md): What the project is, tech stack, quick start, and the **Status** board (the authoritative table of what's shipped vs planned).
- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): Rules for AI agents — DB routing, secrets, the canonical environment-variable table, product copy. Read before changing code.
- [Docs index](./docs/README.md): The L0/L1 map of all documentation, organized by Diátaxis mode.

## Documentation

- [Product direction](./docs/explanation/product-direction.md): Why Macrotide exists — north star, personas, the Learn → Analyze → Research → Select loop, index-purist stance, success signals.
- [Getting started](./docs/tutorials/getting-started.md): Guided first run — clone, demo, first holding, first chat.
- [Local development](./docs/how-to/local-development.md): Env setup, npm scripts, seeding, tests, pre-commit hook.
- [Import a portfolio](./docs/how-to/import-a-portfolio.md): CSV, image OCR, and manual entry.
- [Add a broker connector](./docs/how-to/add-a-broker-connector.md): Write a connector manifest (data-only, with a response `shape`) to support a new broker — no app code.
- [Architecture](./docs/explanation/architecture.md): System shape, request lifecycle, demo/owner DB routing, where everything lives.
- [Balances and History](./docs/explanation/balances-and-history.md): How a Balance and a trade fit together — the one-ledger model, cost-basis-delta math with worked examples, self-healing edits, custom-asset pricing, and the History/entry/Balance vocabulary.
- [Design principles](./docs/explanation/design-principles.md): Secure-by-default, the "Advisor" voice, single-owner → multi-user.
- [Portfolio health](./docs/explanation/portfolio-health.md): Why the Portfolio screen leads with a plain-language headline + named checks instead of a 0–100 grade, and how the diversification check measures underlying concentration (single-name overlap + target-relative region) honestly on partial look-through data.
- [Market data pipeline](./docs/explanation/market-data-pipeline.md): How market.db is fed — the fund-catalog crawl (ELT: sec_raw → transform) vs the NAV/quote series cache (`getCachedSeries`, 24h TTL, depth-aware, upsert-only), the freshness (`refresh-market`) vs coverage (`prewarm-nav`) jobs, and coverage limits (Thai funds deep to SEC's ~5.4y cap; indices/FX/gold still shallow). Links out to provider/quota and schedule facts.
- [Memory feature](./docs/explanation/memory.md): Long-term memory + chat-session lifecycle design.
- [Advisor context model](./docs/explanation/advisor-context.md): What the Advisor knows per turn — the three context channels, the entry-point contract, and the empty-turn recovery.
- [In-chat vision](./docs/explanation/advisor-vision.md): How the Advisor reads images you attach in chat — message flow, the dedicated `VISION_CHAT_MODEL` (free-tier invariant + caps), table-vs-card output, and ephemeral-server / browser-only image storage.
- [Inference strategy](./docs/explanation/inference-strategy.md): How the Advisor stays smart/fast/token-efficient — model routing & tiers, prompt-cache strategy, reasoning-token policy, context loading, tool-result shaping; each lever mapped to the backlog.
- [Decisions log](./docs/explanation/decisions/README.md): Locked decisions and the durable rules behind them (ADR-style).

## Reference

- [Configuration](./docs/reference/configuration.md): How config works; links to the canonical env-var table in AGENTS.md.
- [API routes](./docs/reference/api.md): Catalog of app/api/* route handlers.
- [Data model](./docs/reference/data-model.md): SQLite tables and relationships.
- [Design system](./docs/reference/design-system.md): Real CSS tokens (color/radius/spacing), high-reuse classes, and shared UI components (Modal, Icon, EventLine) copied from production for building UI that matches.
- [Auth & AI providers](./docs/reference/auth-and-providers.md): Passkeys, OpenRouter, rate limiting.
- [Deploy](./docs/how-to/deploy.md): Single-VM (Caddy/systemd) and tailnet deployment.
- [Security policy](./SECURITY.md): Threat model and vulnerability reporting.

## Optional

- [Roadmap (GitHub Project)](https://github.com/users/Sitthinut/projects/2): Forward-looking work as issues grouped by Priority. Product intent + out-of-scope notes live in [product-direction.md](./docs/explanation/product-direction.md). For what's *done*, see the README Status board and the changelog.
- [Changelog](./CHANGELOG.md): Notable changes per release, Keep a Changelog format.
- [Memory-systems research](./docs/explanation/research/memory-systems.md): Prior-art survey behind the memory design.
- [Context-engineering research](./docs/explanation/research/context-engineering.md): Prior-art survey on context engineering for tool-using agents — the Advisor loop, failure modes, and recovery.
- [LLM-platform-primitives research](./docs/explanation/research/llm-platform-primitives.md): Prior-art survey of how Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter expose tool calling, system prompts, reasoning tokens, and citations/structured output.
- [Context-and-caching research](./docs/explanation/research/context-and-caching.md): Prior-art survey on prompt caching (cost/latency math) and context-window management / progressive context loading.
- [Agent-evals research](./docs/explanation/research/agent-evals.md): Prior-art survey on evaluating tool-using agents — the task/harness/grader triple, deterministic vs LLM-judge graders, pass^k reliability, dead-end metrics; the evidence behind scripts/eval.
- [Design-systems research](./docs/explanation/research/design-systems.md): Prior-art survey on keeping a design system consistent and human/AI-foolproof — design tokens (DTCG 2025.10), typed component APIs (cva, discriminated unions), lint/CI gates (Stylelint strict-value), agent guardrails (DESIGN.md, MCP, AGENTS.md), and small-team governance.
